creator: avg@FreeBSD.org
text:
Another question that I'd like to ask is does any popular OS / OS distribution out there use the sample vimrc as the default vimrc?
My impression from using several mainstream Linux distributions is that they use their own custom default vimrc files (installed under /tmp/vimrc, typically).
And I have never encountered setting like those in the sample vimrc, esp. regarding the mouse integration.

Finally, regarding the message that accompanies the commit that introduced the POLA violation.  I do not think that vim actually operates in strict vi compatibility mode without any vimrc.  Also, arrow keys work as expected for me in vim without any vimrc.  Maybe terminal properties play a greater role with these.  However, it's true that syntax highlighting ("color mode") has to be explicitly enabled.
